  
![][image1]

MVP Product Document

**ReviewReply**

AI-Powered Business Review Responder

Maxwize Online Private Limited

Version 1.0  |  February 2026

**Confidential**

# **1\. Executive Summary**

ReviewReply is an AI-powered platform that helps local businesses and multi-location brands respond to Google Business Profile reviews quickly, professionally, and consistently. The platform connects to a business's Google profile, monitors incoming reviews in real-time, and generates personalized, on-brand response drafts that owners can approve with a single click or edit before posting.

Every local business — restaurants, salons, clinics, retail shops, fitness studios, hotels — receives reviews but most either ignore them (damaging their reputation) or spend disproportionate time crafting individual responses. ReviewReply eliminates this friction while maintaining the authentic, personal tone that customers expect.

| Key Numbers Target MVP Build: 2–3 weeks  |  Team: 3 Devs \+ 1 QA \+ 1 PM  |  Pricing: Free (5 replies/mo) \+ $15/mo \+ $39/mo |
| :---- |

# **2\. Problem Statement**

Online reviews are the modern word-of-mouth. 93% of consumers say online reviews influence their purchasing decisions, and Google prioritizes businesses that actively respond to reviews in local search rankings. Yet the vast majority of small businesses struggle with review management.

The problem manifests differently depending on business size: solo owners simply don't have time to craft thoughtful responses; multi-location businesses face an overwhelming volume across dozens of listings; and franchise operators need to maintain a consistent brand voice across independent managers.

## **Pain Points**

* Business owners spend 15–30 minutes per review crafting individual responses, especially for negative reviews

* Unanswered reviews signal to potential customers that the business doesn't care

* Negative reviews without a professional response cause disproportionate reputation damage

* Multi-location businesses have no centralized way to manage reviews across all their Google listings

* Inconsistent tone — different staff members respond differently, damaging brand consistency

* Language barriers: business owners in non-English markets struggle to respond in English (and vice versa)

* No analytics on review sentiment trends over time

# **3\. Market Opportunity**

The addressable market for ReviewReply spans every business with a Google Business Profile. India alone has over 60 million SMBs, with a rapidly growing number establishing Google Business Profiles. The global review management software market is growing at approximately 15% annually.

## **3.1 Target Segments**

| Segment | Examples | Reviews/Month | Willingness to Pay |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Restaurants & Cafes | Local eateries, cloud kitchens, chains | 20–100+ | High — reviews directly impact footfall |
| Health & Wellness | Clinics, dentists, gyms, spas, salons | 10–40 | High — trust is critical for patient/client decisions |
| Retail & Shopping | Boutiques, electronics, furniture stores | 5–30 | Medium — value increases with online competition |
| Hotels & Hospitality | Hotels, resorts, homestays, PGs | 15–80 | Very High — reviews are primary booking factor |
| Professional Services | Lawyers, accountants, agencies, tutors | 3–15 | Medium — fewer reviews but each matters more |
| Automotive | Car dealers, service centers, garages | 10–40 | Medium-High — high-value trust decisions |

## **3.2 Competitive Landscape**

| Competitor | Price | Weakness | Our Advantage |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Birdeye | $299+/mo | Expensive, enterprise-focused, overkill for SMBs | 10x cheaper, focused on the core job |
| Podium | $249+/mo | Complex platform, long onboarding, contracts | Setup in 2 minutes, no contracts |
| Reputation.com | Custom pricing | Enterprise only, not accessible to SMBs | Built for small businesses first |
| Manual replies | Free (time cost) | Inconsistent, time-consuming, often neglected | AI saves 90% of time, always consistent |

# **4\. Target Users**

| Persona | Description | Key Motivation |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Solo Business Owner | Runs a restaurant, salon, or clinic. Wears every hat. Checks reviews on their phone between tasks. | Save time. Look professional. Never miss a review. Handle negative reviews without emotional reaction. |
| Multi-Location Manager | Manages 5–50 locations for a brand or franchise. Drowning in review volume across locations. | Centralized dashboard. Consistent brand voice. Location-level performance tracking. |
| Marketing Agency | Manages online presence for multiple SMB clients. Review management is part of their retainer. | Efficiency at scale. White-label reports for clients. Charge for review management as a service. |
| Office Manager / Staff | Delegated the task of responding to reviews but has no training or guidelines. | AI-generated drafts they can approve. No need to figure out what to say. Confidence in the response. |

# **5\. MVP Feature Set**

The MVP delivers the core review-to-response loop: connect Google Business Profile, receive reviews, generate AI responses, and post with one click. Advanced features like multi-platform support, sentiment analytics, and automated posting are deferred to V2.

## **5.1 Google Business Profile Integration**

* OAuth-based connection to Google Business Profile API (Google My Business API)

* Automatic sync of all existing reviews on first connection

* Real-time monitoring for new reviews via webhook/polling (every 15 minutes)

* Support for multiple business locations under one account

* Display review details: reviewer name, star rating, review text, date, review photos

## **5.2 AI Response Generation**

* Personalized responses that reference specific details mentioned in the review

* Tone matching: professional, warm, apologetic, enthusiastic — adapts to review sentiment

* Business context awareness: understands the type of business and adjusts language accordingly

* Custom brand voice configuration: formal vs. casual, include/exclude owner name, preferred sign-off

* Negative review handling: acknowledges concern, offers resolution path, invites offline conversation

* Multi-language support: generate responses in the same language as the review (Hindi, Kannada, Tamil, English, etc.)

* Three response variants per review: owner picks the best fit or edits any of them

## **5.3 Review Dashboard**

* Unified inbox showing all reviews across all connected locations

* Filter by: star rating, response status (pending/responded/skipped), date range, location

* Color-coded priority: 1-2 stars (red/urgent), 3 stars (amber/attention), 4-5 stars (green/positive)

* One-click approve and post: AI draft is posted directly to Google via API

* Edit before posting: inline editor to modify the AI draft

* Bulk actions: approve all 5-star responses at once, snooze low-priority reviews

* Review count and response rate summary per location

## **5.4 Notifications**

* Email alerts for new reviews (configurable: all reviews, only negative, daily digest)

* WhatsApp notification for negative reviews (1-2 stars) with quick-reply link

* Weekly summary email: new reviews received, responses posted, average rating

## **5.5 Free vs Paid Tiers**

| Feature | Free | Pro ($15/mo) | Business ($39/mo) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| AI replies per month | 5 | 100 | Unlimited |
| Connected locations | 1 | 1 | Up to 10 |
| Response variants | 1 per review | 3 per review | 3 per review |
| Brand voice config | Default only | ✓ Custom | ✓ Per location |
| One-click post to Google | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| WhatsApp alerts | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Review history | 30 days | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| Multi-language replies | English only | ✓ All languages | ✓ All languages |
| Weekly summary email | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Team members | 1 | 1 | 5 |
| Sentiment analytics | ✗ | ✗ (V2) | ✗ (V2) |

# **6\. Tech Stack**

| Layer | Technology | Rationale |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Frontend | Next.js 14 \+ Tailwind CSS | Dashboard-heavy app, SSR for landing page SEO |
| Backend | Next.js API Routes \+ Node.js | Unified codebase, handles OAuth flows and webhooks |
| Database | PostgreSQL (Supabase) | Relational model for reviews, responses, locations, users |
| Auth | Supabase Auth \+ Google OAuth | Google OAuth required for GBP API access |
| Google Integration | Google Business Profile API (v4) | Fetch reviews, post responses, manage locations |
| AI Engine | Claude API (Anthropic) | Response generation with context awareness and tone control |
| Job Queue | BullMQ \+ Upstash Redis | Background review polling, batch AI generation |
| Notifications | Resend (email) \+ WhatsApp Business API / Twilio | Transactional emails and WhatsApp alerts for negative reviews |
| Payments | Stripe / Razorpay | Global \+ India payment support |
| Hosting | Vercel (app) \+ Railway (worker) | Vercel for frontend, Railway for polling/background jobs |
| Monitoring | Sentry \+ Uptime Kuma | Error tracking and uptime monitoring for the platform itself |

# **7\. System Architecture**

## **7.1 High-Level Flow**

1. Business owner signs up and connects their Google Business Profile via OAuth 2.0

2. System fetches all existing reviews and locations from GBP API

3. Background worker polls for new reviews every 15 minutes (or via push notification if available)

4. New review detected → AI generates 1–3 response drafts using Claude API with business context

5. Owner receives notification (email/WhatsApp) with review preview and link to dashboard

6. Owner opens dashboard, reviews the AI draft(s), selects or edits one

7. One-click post: response is submitted to Google via GBP API and marked as responded

8. Weekly summary email sent with stats: reviews received, responded, average rating

## **7.2 AI Response Pipeline**

The response generation engine uses a multi-layer prompt architecture for high-quality, context-aware replies:

* Layer 1 — Business Profile: Business name, type (restaurant/salon/clinic), brand voice settings, preferred language, owner name

* Layer 2 — Review Analysis: Star rating, sentiment classification (positive/neutral/negative/mixed), key topics mentioned (food quality, wait time, staff behavior, pricing, etc.)

* Layer 3 — Response Strategy: Positive reviews get gratitude \+ specific acknowledgment; negative reviews get empathy \+ acknowledgment \+ resolution offer; mixed reviews address both sides

* Layer 4 — Brand Voice: Apply configured tone (formal/friendly/casual), include/exclude owner name, match response language to review language

* Output: 1–3 response variants, each 2–4 sentences, with different approaches (e.g., concise vs. detailed, apologetic vs. solution-focused)

## **7.3 Database Schema (Core Tables)**

| Table | Key Fields | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| users | id, email, name, plan, google\_tokens | UUID, text, text, enum, jsonb | Encrypted Google OAuth tokens |
| locations | id, user\_id, google\_location\_id, name, address, category | UUID, FK, text, text, text, text | Synced from GBP API |
| reviews | id, location\_id, google\_review\_id, reviewer\_name, rating, text, created\_at | UUID, FK, text, text, int, text, timestamp | Synced reviews with dedup |
| responses | id, review\_id, variant\_number, text, status, posted\_at | UUID, FK, int, text, enum, timestamp | Status: draft/approved/posted/skipped |
| brand\_voice | id, user\_id, location\_id, tone, language, owner\_name, sign\_off, custom\_instructions | UUID, FK, FK, enum, text, text, text, text | Per-location brand settings |
| notification\_prefs | id, user\_id, email\_alerts, whatsapp\_alerts, whatsapp\_number, digest\_frequency | UUID, FK, bool, bool, text, enum | User notification config |
| usage\_log | id, user\_id, month, ai\_replies\_used, reviews\_received | UUID, FK, date, int, int | Track usage for tier limits |

# **8\. API Endpoints**

| Method | Endpoint | Auth | Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| GET | /api/auth/google | None | Initiate Google OAuth for GBP access |
| GET | /api/auth/google/callback | None | Handle OAuth callback, store tokens |
| GET | /api/locations | Required | List connected business locations |
| POST | /api/locations/sync | Required | Force sync locations from GBP |
| GET | /api/reviews | Required | List reviews (filterable by location, rating, status) |
| POST | /api/reviews/:id/generate | Required | Generate AI response(s) for a review |
| POST | /api/reviews/:id/respond | Required | Approve and post response to Google |
| PUT | /api/reviews/:id/response | Required | Edit response draft before posting |
| POST | /api/reviews/bulk-approve | Required | Bulk approve AI responses for selected reviews |
| GET | /api/stats/summary | Required | Dashboard stats: review counts, response rates, ratings |
| PUT | /api/settings/brand-voice | Required | Update brand voice configuration |
| PUT | /api/settings/notifications | Required | Update notification preferences |
| POST | /api/billing/subscribe | Required | Create subscription |
| POST | /api/webhooks/stripe | Stripe sig | Handle payment events |

# **9\. Google API Integration**

ReviewReply integrates with the Google Business Profile API (formerly Google My Business API) for reading reviews and posting responses. This requires specific OAuth scopes and API access approval.

## **9.1 Required OAuth Scopes**

* https://www.googleapis.com/auth/business.manage — Read/write access to business information, reviews, and responses

## **9.2 API Operations**

| Operation | API Endpoint | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| List locations | GET accounts/{accountId}/locations | Fetches all business locations |
| List reviews | GET locations/{locationId}/reviews | Paginated, sorted by date |
| Get single review | GET locations/{locationId}/reviews/{reviewId} | Full review details |
| Post response | PUT locations/{locationId}/reviews/{reviewId}/reply | Creates or updates owner reply |
| Delete response | DELETE locations/{locationId}/reviews/{reviewId}/reply | Removes owner reply |

## **9.3 API Access Requirements**

* Google Cloud project with Business Profile API enabled

* OAuth consent screen configured and verified (production access requires Google review)

* Rate limits: 60 requests per minute per project (sufficient for MVP scale)

* Important: Google verification process for OAuth consent screen can take 2–6 weeks — start this in Week 1

# **10\. Sprint Plan (3 Weeks)**

Team allocation: 3 Developers (D1, D2, D3), 1 QA, 1 PM. All development assisted by Claude Code.

## **Week 1: Google Integration \+ AI Engine**

| Owner | Task | Deliverable |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| D1 | Google OAuth flow, GBP API integration (fetch locations, fetch reviews, post responses), token management and refresh | Google integration working end-to-end |
| D2 | AI response engine: Claude API integration, multi-layer prompt architecture, response variant generation, tone/language control | AI generating quality responses |
| D3 | Landing page UI, sign-up/onboarding flow with Google connect, brand voice configuration screen | Landing page \+ onboarding flow |
| PM | Submit Google OAuth consent screen for verification (critical path), define brand voice presets, write AI prompt templates for 6 business categories | Google app submitted \+ prompt specs |
| QA | Set up test framework, create test Google Business accounts, prepare test reviews across categories and languages | Test environment ready |

## **Week 2: Dashboard \+ Notifications**

| Owner | Task | Deliverable |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| D1 | Review dashboard: unified inbox, filters (rating/status/location/date), review detail view with AI response variants | Functional dashboard |
| D2 | Background polling worker (BullMQ), notification system (email via Resend, WhatsApp via Twilio), weekly digest generation | Real-time monitoring \+ alerts |
| D3 | One-click approve UI, inline response editor, bulk approve flow, response posting to Google, stats summary dashboard | Complete response workflow |
| PM | Test AI responses with 50+ real-world reviews, refine prompts for accuracy and tone, prepare demo data for different business types | Validated AI quality |
| QA | Test Google sync reliability, AI response quality across languages, notification delivery, edge cases (empty reviews, photo-only reviews) | Integration QA complete |

## **Week 3: Payments, Polish & Launch**

| Owner | Task | Deliverable |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| D1 | Stripe/Razorpay integration, tier enforcement (reply limits, location limits), subscription management | Payment flow live |
| D2 | Production deployment, error monitoring (Sentry), rate limiting, Google token encryption, security audit | Secure production infra |
| D3 | Responsive design (mobile-first — owners check reviews on phone), loading states, empty states, onboarding tooltips | Polished mobile UX |
| PM | Recruit 5–10 beta businesses (from Maxwize network), collect feedback, prepare launch materials, SEO content | Beta launch complete |
| QA | Full regression, payment testing, cross-browser \+ mobile testing, production smoke tests, load testing, final sign-off | Launch approval |

# **11\. Monetization Strategy**

## **11.1 Revenue Projections (Year 1\)**

ReviewReply has unusually strong retention characteristics: once a business connects their Google profile and starts responding to reviews through the platform, switching costs are high (habit formation \+ historical data). Churn is expected to be low at 3–5% monthly.

| Metric | Month 3 | Month 6 | Month 12 |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Free users | 300 | 1,200 | 4,000 |
| Pro subscribers ($15/mo) | 25 | 100 | 350 |
| Business subscribers ($39/mo) | 5 | 20 | 60 |
| MRR | $570 | $2,280 | $7,590 |
| Annual Run Rate | $6,840 | $27,360 | $91,080 |

## **11.2 Growth Channels**

* Maxwize's existing e-commerce clients as first adopters (Anand Sweets, Adukale, etc. — all have Google Business Profiles)

* Local business communities: BNI chapters, Chamber of Commerce, local business WhatsApp groups

* Content marketing: "How to respond to negative Google reviews" — extremely high search volume, low competition

* Partnership with Zoho / Shopify ecosystems — complementary tool for businesses already using these platforms

* Referral program: Give 1 month free for every referred business that subscribes

* Agency channel: marketing agencies resell as part of their reputation management service

## **11.3 Unit Economics**

ReviewReply has strong unit economics due to low per-user infrastructure costs:

| Cost Component | Per User/Month (Pro Tier) |
| :---- | :---- |
| Claude API (avg 100 responses × \~500 tokens each) | \~$0.50 |
| Google API calls | \~$0.00 (free tier sufficient) |
| Hosting (proportional) | \~$0.30 |
| Email/WhatsApp notifications | \~$0.20 |
| Total cost per Pro user | \~$1.00/month |
| Revenue per Pro user | $15.00/month |
| Gross margin | \~93% |

# **12\. Success Metrics (MVP)**

| Metric | Target (30 days) | Target (90 days) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Connected businesses | 30+ | 200+ |
| AI responses generated | 500+ | 5,000+ |
| Responses posted to Google | 300+ | 3,500+ |
| Approve rate (AI draft accepted as-is) | \> 50% | \> 70% |
| Free → Paid conversion | 5% | 8% |
| Monthly churn (paid) | \< 8% | \< 5% |
| Average time to respond (via app) | \< 30 seconds | \< 15 seconds |
| NPS score | \> 40 | \> 55 |

# **13\. V2 Roadmap (Post-MVP)**

| Feature | Description | Priority |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Auto-respond mode | Automatically post AI responses for 4-5 star reviews without manual approval (configurable) | High |
| Sentiment analytics | Dashboard showing sentiment trends, common topics, rating trajectory over time | High |
| Multi-platform support | Add Yelp, TripAdvisor, Facebook, Zomato, Swiggy reviews alongside Google | High |
| Review request campaigns | Send SMS/WhatsApp to happy customers asking for a Google review | Medium |
| White-label for agencies | Agencies rebrand the dashboard for their clients, manage all client reviews from one account | Medium |
| AI-powered review insights | Monthly AI report: "Customers love your desserts but frequently complain about parking" | Medium |
| Competitor review monitoring | Track competitors' Google reviews and average ratings alongside your own | Low |
| Review widget for website | Embed a beautiful review widget on the business website showing latest Google reviews | Low |
| Zapier / Make integration | Connect ReviewReply events to 1000+ apps (new review → Slack, CRM update, etc.) | Low |

# **14\. Risks & Mitigations**

| Risk | Impact | Likelihood | Mitigation |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Google OAuth verification delay | Launch blocked by 2–6 week approval | High | Submit in Week 1, use test accounts during dev, have backup plan for limited beta |
| Google API policy changes | Integration breaks or requires rework | Medium | Abstract Google layer, monitor API changelog, maintain relationship with Google partner support |
| AI generates inappropriate responses | Business posts something embarrassing | Low | Always require human approval in MVP (no auto-post), content safety filters, review before post |
| Low SMB tech adoption | Businesses struggle with Google OAuth flow | Medium | Simplified onboarding with video guides, WhatsApp-based support, offer setup assistance |
| Review platform adds native AI replies | Google builds this feature natively | Low-Medium | Differentiate with multi-platform support (V2), brand voice control, and analytics — Google's native solution would likely be generic |
| WhatsApp Business API costs | Notification costs eat margins at scale | Low | Make WhatsApp notifications a paid feature, use email as free default, batch notifications |

End of Document  —  ReviewReply MVP v1.0  
Maxwize Web Solutions  |  February 2026
